title: Buttery Malaysian Lamb Satay
description: A comforting and juicy Malaysian dish featuring lamb loin chops, cooked in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot to perfection.
instructions: Season the lamb loin chops with garam masala, cumin, coriander, turmeric, salt, and black pepper.
In a small bowl, mix together the coconut milk, butter, and sambal oelek.
Heat the ttukbaegi earthenware pot over medium heat.
Add the lamb loin chops to the pot and cook for 2 minutes on each side, or until browned.
Pour the coconut milk mixture over the lamb loin chops and bring to a simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven. Cook for 20 minutes, or until the lamb is cooked to your liking.
Meanwhile, toast the peanuts in a dry pan over medium heat, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly browned.
Garnish the lamb with toasted peanuts, fresh cilantro, and serve with lime wedges on the side.
Let the lamb rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain.
Serve the satay hot, with your choice of rice, noodles, or vegetables on the side.
Adjust the seasoning as needed, to taste.
Garnish with additional cilantro and peanuts, if desired.
Serve with additional sambal oelek on the side, for added heat, if desired.
